name: Memphis.dev Modern Message Broker API
what_it_does: Modern open-source message broker with REST and SDK APIs — enabling developers to build event-driven applications with a Kafka-alternative that offers a developer-friendly UI, dead-letter station (DLS) for failed messages, message schema enforcement, embedded monitoring, and consumer group management, with cloud-hosted and self-hosted options.
use_cases: Building event-driven microservices via Memphis SDK for reliable async message passing
Managing dead-letter messages via Memphis DLS for failed message handling and reprocessing
Enforcing message schemas via Memphis for data quality in streaming pipelines
Monitoring message flow and consumer lag via Memphis UI for observability
Migrating from Kafka to Memphis for simpler operations without ZooKeeper/KRaft complexity
not_for: Teams needing Kafka-compatible protocol (Memphis has its own protocol, not Kafka-compatible)
Extreme throughput (millions of msg/sec) without custom optimization (Kafka/Redpanda for highest throughput)
Teams locked into Kafka ecosystem tooling (Kafka Streams, Kafka Connect — use Kafka/Redpanda)
best_when: A development team wants Kafka-like streaming with a dramatically better developer experience — where Memphis provides built-in DLS, schema enforcement, UI monitoring, and simpler ops without Kafka's operational overhead.
avoid_when: You need Kafka protocol compatibility, have existing Kafka ecosystem investments, or need maximum throughput for massive-scale pipelines.

auth.notes: Username + password + connection token for SDK connections. REST API via JWT token (obtained via login). Memphis Cloud: managed credentials via dashboard. Self-hosted: admin-configured users. Role-based access control (RBAC): Producer, Consumer, Admin. Separate credentials per station (message queue). No OAuth2. Audit logs for all operations.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free tier: 5GB/month, 1 broker; Memphis Cloud managed
pricing.paid_tiers: Starter $20/month; Pro $100/month; Enterprise custom; Self-hosted free (BSL license)
pricing.notes: Israeli company (Tel Aviv). Founded 2021. Raised $8.2M seed (2022). Go-based broker. Business Source License (BSL 1.1) for newer versions — converts to Apache 2.0 after 4 years. Early versions (pre-1.0) were Apache 2.0. Memphis Cloud: managed service. Self-hosted: Kubernetes or Docker Compose. Built-in DLS (Dead-Letter Station) — unique feature vs RabbitMQ/Kafka. Schema Registry built-in (Avro, JSON Schema, Protobuf). Monitoring UI built-in (no separate Grafana needed). Functions feature for serverless message processing. Connectors for Kafka, MySQL, MongoDB, S3. Discord community (~3,000 members). GitHub: 3,000+ stars. Note: company pivoted in 2024 to SuperStream (Kafka optimization layer).

performance.rate_limits: Free tier: 5GB/month; paid plans vary by volume

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: SDK-based connections (not REST by default). REST API via JWT. RBAC. Dead-Letter Station for failed messages. Schema enforcement. Cloud and self-hosted options. BSL license concern for commercial self-hosting. Company pivoted to SuperStream in 2024.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Message consumption: at-least-once delivery — consumers must be idempotent. Message ID available for deduplication. DLS for failed message tracking. Producer deduplication via idempotency_window config.
known_agent_gotchas: Company pivoted to SuperStream in 2024 — Memphis.dev may have reduced development focus
BSL 1.1 license for self-hosted (newer versions) — not true open source for commercial use
SDK-based protocol (not REST) for producers/consumers — REST API is admin/management only
Dead-Letter Station (DLS) is Memphis-specific — no equivalent in standard messaging APIs
RBAC credentials required: username + password + connection token (3 credentials for SDK)
5GB/month free tier limit — insufficient for high-volume production workloads

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. RBAC with producer/consumer/admin roles. JWT for REST API. BSL license (not OSS — reduces auditability for self-hosted commercial). US/EU data residency (cloud). Israeli company. Audit logs. No OAuth2. SDK credentials: username + password + connection token.
